$comment: Course schema for the Pre-Sold Author Package's methodology component. The package itself is a ProfessionalService (see 03-professionalservice.json), not a course, so the Course entity here represents the METHODOLOGY page (/the-method) as a structured educational program that visitors can learn from. This is a legitimate use of Course: Google recognizes Course markup on methodology pages as long as the entity is educational content the visitor can consume, even if the actual buyable product is a service. If the Course rendering feels forced during a final review, ship this schema only inside the @graph for /the-method and drop it elsewhere. Do not attach Course markup to the /the-package product page — that would be a Google structured data violation.
name: The 180-Day Premium Authority Launch Method
description: The documented methodology PodcastNetwork.org uses to deliver a book, a podcast, a Google Knowledge Panel, and a pre-sold audience of 5,000+ readers on a single 180-day clock. Six months, four workstreams, one operator, one manuscript handoff at Day 90, one launch at Day 180.
teaches: How the four pillars of a premium authority launch (book, podcast, knowledge panel, pre-sold audience) compound when built in parallel
How to compress a knowledge panel from a 12-to-24-month standalone engagement to a 180-day parallel workstream
How to build a pre-sold audience of 5,000+ through a sequenced 12-to-15 podcast guest tour
How to run the Legacy Publishing JV handoff at Day 90 without stalling the pre-sell sequence

hasCourseInstance.name: Pre-Sold Author Package cohort
hasCourseInstance.courseMode: Blended (asynchronous documentation plus synchronous operator delivery)

hasPart.name: Module 1: Foundation (Days 1 to 30)
hasPart.description: Kickoff, editorial planning, brand and positioning work. Podcast launches inside the network by Day 30. Knowledge panel seeding begins on Day 1. Guest-booking outreach starts.

hasPart.name: Module 2: Build (Days 30 to 90)
hasPart.description: Manuscript in production with Legacy. Podcast episodes shipping weekly. Knowledge panel signals accumulating. First wave of guest appearances confirmed and recorded, feeding the list build.

hasPart.name: Module 3: The Pre-Sell (Days 90 to 180)
hasPart.description: Manuscript through five-pass edit. Cover and interior layout finalized. Knowledge panel typically indexed and maturing on Google. Guest appearances peak, list crosses target, waitlist opens for pre-orders.

hasPart.name: Module 4: Launch (Day 180)
hasPart.description: Book on sale. Podcast at scale. Knowledge panel live. Audience already opted in and already paying. Legacy runs retail. PodcastNetwork.org runs launch-week metrics and the 30-day post-launch report.
